## Example Reply Patterns

> Hi Tzvi, good morning!
> 
> Thanks for your patience as I worked on this. 
> 
> After taking a look at the plans, I believe on low frequency bands (like B13 for example) 1 antenna will be enough to cover each floor, but it is unlikely to be the case for higher frequency bands.
> 
> The G41 can boost 2 bands simultaneously, as long as they are available outside where the donor antenna is placed.
> 
> Low frequency bands are great for phone calls and SMS messaging, but offer a reduced internet speed. Higher frequency bands cannot penetrate walls very well but offer higher inernet speed.
> 
> Please let me know if that makes sense or you have any questions!
> 
> Regards,

> Hi Tzvi, good morning!
> 
> Sorry got your message after I left the office yesterday. I just looked at the reading and they look usable, however the signal strength is too low for a 65 or even a 75 dB booster. For those readings I think the best bet would be a 100 dB gain booster from Cel-Fi; either the all carrier G43 or the single carrier G41.
> 
> For reference; for a 75 dB gain booster we would be looking for a signal with RSRP better than around -85 dBm.
> 
> Please let me know if you have any questions or if you'd like to hop on a call any time today.
> 
> Best regards,

> Hi Tzvi,
> 
> Thanks again for your time over the phone!
> 
> Here's what we disussed for that ~5,000 sq ft office:
> 
> (A) Cel-Fi G43: ~$7,000 for all hardware components
> 
> (B) Verizon 4G LTE Network Extender 3 for Enterprise: ~$4,000 for all hardware components
> 
> (C) Verizon 5G Network Extender 4 for Enterprise: ~7,500 for all hardware components
> 
> WeBoost options: (low gain)
> 
> (A) weBoost Office 200 - $1,699.99 (without add-ons)
> (B) weBoost Installed | Office 200 - $3,799.99
> (C) weBoost Installed | Office 300 - $3,799.99
> 
> Please let me know if you have any questions at all!
> 
> Best regards,
